steps.title: Identify the initial value
steps.content: At $t=0$ the account balance equals the initial deposit $\$36{,}100.00$. Any equation $A(t)=A_0\cdot b^t$ must satisfy $A(0)=A_0=36{,}100.00$, so the coefficient in front must be $36{,}100.00$.

steps.title: Identify the growth factor
steps.content: The balance grows from $36{,}100$ to $68{,}071.93$ over $13$ years, so:$$b^{13}=\frac{68{,}071.93}{36{,}100}\approx 1.88565$$Then $b\approx 1.88565^{1/13}\approx 1.05$. The annual multiplier is $1.05$, matching 5% growth.

steps.title: Compare with each choice
steps.content:
• A) $A(t)=36{,}100.00(1.05)^{t}$: $A(0)=36{,}100$ and $A(13)=36{,}100(1.05)^{13}\approx 68{,}071.93$. Matches.
  • B) $A(t)=31{,}971.93(1.05)^{t}$: $A(0)=31{,}971.93\neq 36{,}100$. Eliminated.
  • C) $A(t)=31{,}971.93(0.05)^{t}$: wrong initial value and $0.05$ is decay, not growth. Eliminated.
  • D) $A(t)=36{,}100.00(0.05)^{t}$: $A(13)=36{,}100(0.05)^{13}\approx 0$, not $68{,}071.93$. Eliminated.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
